perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflicts-of-law principles. Each party hereto irrevocably and unconditionally (a) consents to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of New York and of the United States of America located in the State of New York for any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement (and irrevocably and unconditionally agrees not to commence any such action, suit, or proceeding except in such courts), (b) waives any objection to the laying of venue of any such action, suit or proceeding in any such courts and (c) waives and agrees not to plead or claim that any such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflicts-of-law principles. Each party hereto irrevocably and unconditionally (a) consents to submit to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Delaware for any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement (and irrevocably and unconditionally agrees not to commence any such action, suit, or proceeding except in such courts), (b) waives any objection to the laying of venue of any such action, suit or proceeding in any such courts and (c) waives and agrees not to plead or claim that any such action, suit or proceeding brought in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum.
perturbation.explanation: The jurisdiction for disputes has been changed from New York to Delaware. This introduces ambiguity and potential conflicts with the original intent of the parties.
perturbation.law_explanation: Changing the venue to Delaware, despite the companies being based/operating primarily in New York, could violate 28 U.S.C. § 1391 which dictates venue based on where the defendant resides or where the cause of action arose. If the cause of action arises in NY, this clause moving it to DE could be deemed unenforceable.

Venue generally (a) Applicability of Section .-Except as otherwise provided by law- (1) this section shall govern the venue of all civil actions brought in district courts of the United States; and (2) the proper venue for a civil action shall be determined without regard to whether the action is local or transitory in nature. (b) Venue in General .-A civil action may be brought in- (1) a judicial district in which a
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the jurisdiction from New York to Delaware. The Law Explanation argues this could violate 28 U.S.C. § 1391 if the cause of action arises in New York, as it dictates venue based on where the defendant resides or where the cause of action arose. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing 28 USC 1391, defines it as concerning "Venue generally," and states that "this section shall govern the venue of all civil actions brought in district courts of the United States." The change from NY to DE introduces a structural flaw by deviating from a venue that might be legally mandated under 28 U.S.C. § 1391, if the cause of action occurred in NY.

perturbation.original_text: You agree that certain Confidential Information may be deemed material non-public information under applicable securities laws. Without limitation of the other restrictions on use or transfer of Confidential Information described herein, you agree that while such information remains non-public, you will not engage, and will direct your Representatives not to engage, in any transactions in the securities of the Company in a manner which would constitute a violation of such laws.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree that certain Confidential Information may be deemed material non-public information under applicable securities laws. Without limitation of the other restrictions on use or transfer of Confidential Information described herein, you agree that while such information remains non-public, you will engage, and will direct your Representatives to engage, in any transactions in the securities of the Company in a manner which would constitute a violation of such laws.
perturbation.explanation: The text has been modified to state that you *will* engage in transactions that violate securities laws, directly contradicting the original intent and potentially violating insider trading laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 78j - Manipulative and deceptive devices
perturbation.law_explanation: By stating that the recipient *will* engage in transactions violating securities laws, the agreement now promotes actions that are illegal under 15 U.S.C. § 78j which prohibits the use of manipulative and deceptive devices in contravention of SEC rules.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 78j - Manipulative and deceptive devices | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 78j - Manipulative and deceptive devices U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next It shall be unlawful for any person , directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce or of the mails, or of any facility of any national securities exchange— (a) (1) To effect a short sale , or to use or employ any stop-loss order in connection with the purchase or sale, of any security other than a government security, in contravention of such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors. (2) Paragraph (1) of this subsection shall not apply to security futures products . (b) To use or employ, in connection with the purchase or sale of any security registered on a national securities exchange or any security not so registered, or any securities-based swap agreement [1] any manipulative or deceptive device or contrivance in contravention of such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors. (c) (1) To effect, accept, or facilitate a transaction involving the loan or borrowing of securities in contravention of such rules and regulations as the Commission may prescribe as necessary or appropriate in the public interest or for the protection of investors. (2) Nothing in paragraph (1) may be construed to limit the authority of the appropriate Federal banking agency (as defined in section 1813(q) of title 12 ), the National Credit Union Administration , or any other Federal department or agency having a responsibility under Federal law to prescribe rules or regulations restricting transactions involving the loan or borrowing of securities in or
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by stating that the recipient *will* engage in transactions that violate securities laws, instead of *will not*. This directly promotes illegal actions, violating 15 U.S.C. § 78j, as highlighted in both scraped snippets. Both snippets state that it is unlawful for any person to use any means of interstate commerce to employ any manipulative or deceptive device, which the modified text now encourages. The change from 'will not' to 'will' fundamentally alters the agreement from prohibiting illegal activity to promoting it, which creates a clear and direct conflict with the law.

perturbation.original_text: The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you agree, at the Company’s election, to either promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession or your Representatives’ possession, and you and your Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you or your Representatives based on the Confidential Information shall be destroyed, and you shall delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such destruction or deletion shall be 2 required where prohibited by law, regulation, regulatory process or proceeding, or stock exchange regulation, or where the Confidential Information is part of the record of proceedings of your Board of Directors; provided , that any such Confidential Information so retained shall continue to be held confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may elect at any time to terminate further access by you to the Confidential Information. Following any written request by the Company, you agree, at the Company’s election, to either promptly redeliver to the Company or destroy all written Confidential Information in your possession or your Representatives’ possession, and you and your Representatives will retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. You further agree that all documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by you or your Representatives based on the Confidential Information shall be retained, and you shall not delete, to the extent reasonably practicable, all Confidential Information from any computer or other electronic device. Notwithstanding the foregoing, no such destruction or deletion shall be 2 required where prohibited by law, regulation, regulatory process or proceeding, or stock exchange regulation, or where the Confidential Information is part of the record of proceedings of your Board of Directors; provided , that any such Confidential Information so retained shall continue to be held confidential in accordance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires destruction of confidential information; the modification changes this to require retaining the information. This can be problematic under certain circumstances and depending on the information, as it may not comply with data retention or privacy laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 17 CFR § 240.17a-4 - Records to be preserved by certain members, brokers and dealers
perturbation.law_explanation: While seemingly innocuous to *retain* documents, 17 CFR § 240.17a-4 outlines specific record retention requirements for brokers and dealers. This modified clause, forcing retention without regard to the specific requirements and timeframes outlined in the regulation, can lead to non-compliance. The modification lacks the necessary conditions and limits specified in the law, and therefore contradicts it.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 240.17a-4 - Records to be preserved by certain exchange members, brokers and dealers. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.17a-4 - Records to be preserved by certain exchange members, brokers and dealers. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.17a-4 Records to be preserved by certain exchange members, brokers and dealers. This section applies to the following types of entities: A member of a national securities exchange who transacts a business in securities directly with others than members of a national securities exchange ; a broker or dealer who transacts a business in securities through the medium of a member of a national securities exchange ; a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer as that term is defined in § 240.3b-12 , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78 o ); a security-based swap dealer registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78 o -10) that is also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act; and a major security-based swap participant registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act that is also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act. Section 240.18a-6 (rather than this section) applies to the following types of entities: A security-based swap dealer registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act that is not also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act; and a major security-based swap participant registered pursuant to section 15F of the Act that is not also a broker or dealer, including an OTC derivatives dealer , registered pursuant to section 15 of the Act. (a) Every member , broker or dealer subject to § 240.17a-3 must preserve for a period of not less than 6 years, the first two years
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the legal obligation to preserve records, as outlined in 17 CFR § 240.17a-4. The original text mandates the destruction of confidential information, aligning with potential regulations on data disposal. However, the changed text requires retaining all copies, extracts, and reproductions, potentially conflicting with the record retention requirements stipulated in the law. The scraped snippet 2 from 17 CFR § 240.17a-4 states that certain entities must 'preserve for a period of not less than 6 years' records. The modified contract forces retention of documents, regardless of whether they fall under the purview and timeframe of the regulation, potentially leading to non-compliance. This directly opposes the regulation and leads to a clear non-compliance with the law.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto and may be modified only by a separate written agreement executed by you and the Company expressly modifying this Agreement. This Agreement supersedes and cancels any and all prior agreements between the parties hereto, express or implied, relating to the Transaction; provided that the Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement dated as of November 13, 2009 between the parties hereto 4 shall remain in full force and effect with respect to the matters contemplated thereby.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto and may be modified verbally by any employee of either party. This Agreement supersedes and cancels any and all prior agreements between the parties hereto, express or implied, relating to the Transaction; provided that the Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement dated as of November 13, 2009 between the parties hereto 4 shall remain in full force and effect with respect to the matters contemplated thereby.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires written modification, protecting against misunderstandings. Changing it to allow verbal modifications by *any* employee opens the agreement to ambiguity and disputes about its terms and potentially violates the Statute of Frauds.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY Gen. Oblig. Law § 5-701 requires certain agreements, particularly those not performable within one year, to be in writing. By allowing verbal modifications by *any* employee, this clause creates a scenario where modifications to an agreement that should be in writing are not, potentially rendering them unenforceable. This contradicts the statute's requirement for a written agreement for specific types of contracts.

Skip to main content Legislation Search OpenLegislation Statutes Search Term Search icon Search The Laws of New York Consolidated Laws of New York CHAPTER 24-A General Obligations ARTICLE 5 Creation, Definition and Enforcement of Contractual Obligations TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability up TITLE 7 Requirements of Writing, Execution or Acknowledgment For Effectiveness or Enforceability next SECTION 5-702 Requirements for use of plain language in consumer transactions This entry was published on 2014-09-22 The selection dates indicate all change milestones for the entire volume, not just the location being viewed. Specifying a milestone date will retrieve the most recent version of the location before that date. See most recent version before or on: 2014-09-22 2015-10-09 2017-10-27 2018-04-20 2018-07-06 2018-07-13 2018-08-31 2019-01-11 2019-01-18 2019-06-28 2019-07-05 2019-07-19 2019-08-16 2019-10-18 2019-11-22 2019-12-13 2020-02-21 2020-04-10 2020-12-18 2021-01-08 2021-04-02 2021-04-09 2021-06-18 2021-09-24 2021-12-31 2022-02-25 2022-03-04 2022-12-30 2023-01-06 2023-10-27 2023-11-26 2024-12-20 2025-01-03 Share Facebook Email SECTION 5-701 Agreements required to be in writing General Obligations (GOB) CHAPTER 24-A, ARTICLE 5, TITLE 7 § 5-701. Agreements required to be in writing. a. Every agreement, promise or undertaking is void, unless it or some note or memorandum thereof be in writing, and subscribed by the party to be charged therewith, or by his lawful agent, if such agreement, promise or undertaking: 1. By its terms is not to be performed within one year from the making thereof or the performance of which is not to be completed before the end of a lifetime; 2. Is a special promise to answer for the debt, default or miscarriage of another person; 3. Is made in consideration of marriage, except mutual promises to marry; 5. Is a subsequent or new promise to pay a debt discharged in bankruptcy; 6. Notwithstanding section 2-201 of the uniform commercial code, if the goods be sold at public auction, and the auctioneer at the time of the sale, enters in a sale book, a memorandum specifying the nature and price of the property sold, the terms of the sale, the name of the purchaser, and the name of the person on whose account the sale was made, such memorandum is equivalent in effect to a note of the contract or sale, subscribed by the party to be charged therewith; 9. Is a contract to assign or an assignment, with or without consideration to the promisor, of a life or health or accident insurance policy, or a promise, with or without consideration to the promisor, to name a beneficiary of any such policy. This provision shall not apply to a policy of industrial life or health or accident insurance. 10. Is a contract to pay compensation for services rendered in negotiating a loan, or in negotiating the purchase, sale, exchange, renting or leasing of any real estate or interest therein, or of a business opportunity, business, its good will, inventory, fixtures or an interest therein, including a majority of the voting stock interest in a corporation and including the creating of a partnership interest.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows verbal modifications to the contract, directly contradicting NY Gen. Oblig. Law § 5-701, as explained. The law snippet states: 'Every agreement, promise or undertaking is void, unless it or some note or memorandum thereof be in writing, and subscribed by the party to be charged therewith...1. By its terms is not to be performed within one year from the making thereof...' The original contract required modifications to be in writing, aligning with the statute, but the changed contract explicitly allows verbal modifications. This creates a contradiction because agreements that are not performable within a year must be in writing to be valid under the statute, and the changed text opens the door to unenforceable verbal modifications of such agreements, therefore a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (a) the closing of the Transaction contemplated by this Agreement, and (b) one (1) year after the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall terminate upon the earlier to occur of (a) the closing of the Transaction contemplated by this Agreement, and (b) ten (10) years after the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Extending the termination date significantly changes the agreement's duration. This prolonged confidentiality period, especially if indefinite information is covered, can raise enforceability issues, particularly concerning trade secrets or non-compete aspects that might unduly restrict competition. Furthermore, Verizon is based in New York, which has specific requirements around non-competes, which could be triggered depending on how this confidential information is used.
perturbation.law_explanation: While this is a confidentiality agreement and not directly a non-compete, if the confidential information clause is so broad as to effectively prevent future employment, the longer term (ten years vs. one) could run afoul of NY Labor Law § 201-g, which limits the enforceability of non-compete agreements. A ten-year confidentiality period could be seen as an unreasonable restraint on trade, indirectly violating the spirit of the non-compete law.
perturbation.justification: The original contract stipulates a one-year termination, while the modified version extends this to ten years. The Law Explanation suggests that a ten-year confidentiality period could be viewed as an unreasonable restraint on trade, potentially conflicting with the spirit of non-compete laws, particularly NY Labor Law § 201-g. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 doesn't directly mention non-compete enforceability, it points to NY Labor Law, providing a basis for the potential contradiction. The extended duration introduces a structural flaw by creating a potentially unenforceable or illegal obligation, especially if the confidential information clause is broad enough to impede future employment, satisfying the conditions for a legal contradiction under Structural Flaws.
